banner: Week 24 · Special Right Triangles — 45-45-90 & hyp = leg√2
essential_question: If a right triangle's two legs are exactly equal, is there a single multiplication — not a full Pythagorean computation — that gives you the hypotenuse every time?
objective: Identify a 45-45-90 (isosceles right) triangle and its constant 1:1:√2 side ratio, find the hypotenuse given a leg using hyp = leg√2, and find a leg given the hypotenuse by dividing by √2 and rationalizing the denominator.

content.prompt: A square patio tile has a side of 6 ft. Without measuring it directly, can you find the length of its diagonal? You already know the two triangles a diagonal cuts a square into are right triangles — what's special about THESE right triangles that a generic one doesn't have?

agenda.title: I do — One Ratio, Every 45-45-90 Triangle

content.heading: hyp = leg × √2, in both directions
bullets: A 45-45-90 triangle has one right angle and two 45° angles — since the angles opposite the legs are equal, the legs themselves are congruent, making it an isosceles right triangle.
Every 45-45-90 triangle shares the same ratio of sides: leg : leg : hypotenuse = 1 : 1 : √2, no matter how big or small the triangle is.
The rule comes straight from the Pythagorean theorem: leg² + leg² = hyp² → 2·leg² = hyp² → hyp = leg√2.
Given a leg, multiply by √2 to get the hypotenuse — one multiplication, no separate squaring/adding/rooting.
Given the hypotenuse, divide by √2 to get the leg, then rationalize the denominator (Lesson 19's skill): leg = hyp/√2 = (hyp√2)/2.

agenda.title: Explore — Drag the Leg, Watch the Hypotenuse

content.heading: One handle, both legs, one ratio
content.note: On the sim, drag the gold handle at the top of the vertical leg. Because both non-right angles stay fixed at 45°, dragging it changes BOTH legs together, keeping them congruent — watch the readout update the leg, the equal leg, and the hypotenuse as an exact radical AND a decimal. Try to land on a leg of exactly 1, then a leg of exactly 5 — does the hypotenuse always land on leg√2?

agenda.title: You do — Find the Missing Sides

content.preview: Build session: fold a square along its diagonal and measure a real 45-45-90 set-square to confirm hyp = leg√2 against reality.

success_criteria.text: I can identify a 45-45-90 (isosceles right) triangle and state its constant 1:1:√2 side ratio
I can find the hypotenuse of a 45-45-90 triangle given a leg, using hyp = leg√2
I can find a leg of a 45-45-90 triangle given the hypotenuse, rationalizing the denominator

questions.prompt: In a 45-45-90 triangle, the two sides that meet at the right angle (the legs) are always…
options: impossible to compare without a protractor
questions.explain: Both non-right angles measure 45°, and equal angles face equal sides — so the two legs opposite them must be congruent. That's why it's also called an isosceles right triangle.

questions.prompt: The ratio of leg : leg : hypotenuse in every 45-45-90 triangle is…
questions.explain: leg² + leg² = hyp² gives hyp = leg√2, so the hypotenuse is always √2 times a leg — the ratio 1 : 1 : √2 never changes, no matter the triangle's size.

questions.prompt: A 45-45-90 triangle has a leg of length 7. Find the length of the other leg and the hypotenuse.
questions.explain: The legs of a 45-45-90 triangle are always congruent, so the other leg is also 7; hyp = leg × √2 = 7√2.

questions.prompt: On the grid, draw a 45-45-90 triangle with legs of 4 units each, mark both 45° angles and the right angle, and label the hypotenuse using hyp = leg√2.
questions.answer: An isosceles right triangle with legs 4 and 4, the right angle marked, both 45° angles marked, and the hypotenuse labeled 4√2.
questions.explain: Rubric: a right triangle with two congruent legs of 4 and all three angles marked (1 pt) + the hypotenuse correctly computed and labeled 4√2 (1 pt).

questions.prompt: A 45-45-90 triangle has a leg of length 3√2. Find the other leg and the hypotenuse, simplifying your answer completely.
questions.explain: hyp = (3√2)(√2) = 3 × 2 = 6 — the radical disappears completely because √2 × √2 = 2 exactly.

questions.prompt: A 45-45-90 triangle has a hypotenuse of length 10. Find the length of each leg, rationalizing the denominator.
questions.explain: leg = 10/√2 = (10/√2)(√2/√2) = 10√2/2 = 5√2 — the denominator has to be rationalized, not left as 10/√2.

questions.prompt: Exit: a square tile has a side of 6 inches. Without doing a full Pythagorean computation, find the length of its diagonal, and name the rule you used.
questions.answer: 6√2 inches — using hyp = leg√2, since the diagonal of a square splits it into two 45-45-90 triangles with legs equal to the square's side.
questions.explain: The square's diagonal is the hypotenuse of a 45-45-90 triangle whose legs are the square's sides, so diagonal = side × √2 = 6√2.
